chunks.title: Command to Take Census and Leaders Named
chunks.reason: Yahweh commands the census and lists the specific leaders appointed for each tribe.

chunks.title: Execution of the Census Order
chunks.reason: Narrative describing Moses and Aaron assembling the congregation to register the men.

chunks.title: Census Results by Tribe
chunks.reason: A repetitive catalog listing the head count for each of the twelve tribes.

chunks.title: Total Count and Exclusion of Levites
chunks.reason: Summary of the total number of men and the specific note that Levites were excluded.

chunks.title: Instructions for the Tribe of Levi
chunks.reason: Specific legal instructions regarding the Levites' duties with the tabernacle and their camp placement.

chunks.title: Completion of the Census
chunks.reason: Concluding statement confirming the Israelites followed Yahweh's command.

chunks.title: Camp of Judah
chunks.reason: Divine instruction on the eastern camp arrangement and military count.

chunks.title: Camp of Reuben
chunks.reason: Divine instruction on the southern camp arrangement and military count.

chunks.title: Levites in Midst
chunks.reason: Instruction regarding the placement of the Levites and the tent of meeting.

chunks.title: Camp of Ephraim
chunks.reason: Divine instruction on the western camp arrangement and military count.

chunks.title: Camp of Dan
chunks.reason: Divine instruction on the northern camp arrangement and military count.

chunks.title: Summary of Camps
chunks.reason: Total census count and narrative conclusion of the Israelites' obedience.

chunks.title: Genealogy of Aaron and Moses
chunks.reason: Introduces the sons of Aaron and notes the death of Nadab and Abihu.

chunks.title: Appointment of Levites to Aaron
chunks.reason: Divine command to set apart the tribe of Levi for priestly service.

chunks.title: Theological Basis for Levites
chunks.reason: Explains the substitution of Levites for the firstborn based on the Exodus.

chunks.title: Enumeration of Levi's Sons
chunks.reason: Lists the three main families and their sub-families.

chunks.title: Gershonites Census and Duties
chunks.reason: Details the count, camp location, leader, and specific responsibilities of the Gershonites.

chunks.title: Kohathites Census and Duties
chunks.reason: Details the count, camp location, leader, and specific responsibilities of the Kohathites.

chunks.title: Merarites Census and Duties
chunks.reason: Details the count, camp location, leader, and specific responsibilities of the Merarites.

chunks.title: Camp Arrangement and Total Count
chunks.reason: Describes the placement of Moses and Aaron and summarizes the total number of Levites.

chunks.title: Census of Firstborns
chunks.reason: Narrates the counting of Israel's firstborn males to compare with the Levites.

chunks.title: Redemption of Firstborns
chunks.reason: Establishes the redemption price for excess firstborns and records the payment.

chunks.title: Service of the Kohathites
chunks.reason: Divine instruction detailing the duties and census criteria for the Kohathite clan regarding the holy objects.

chunks.title: Service of the Gershonites
chunks.reason: Divine instruction detailing the duties and census criteria for the Gershonite clan regarding the tabernacle coverings.

chunks.title: Service of the Merarites
chunks.reason: Divine instruction detailing the duties and census criteria for the Merarite clan regarding the structural elements.

chunks.title: Census Results of the Levites
chunks.reason: Narrative record of the census execution and final tallies for all three Levitical families.

chunks.title: Leapers Sent Outside the Camp
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the command and execution regarding unclean persons.

chunks.title: Law of Restitution for Sins
chunks.reason: Divine instruction on confessing sins and making financial restitution to victims or the priest.

chunks.title: Law of Jealousy for Suspected Adultery
chunks.reason: Detailed legal procedure and ritual for testing a wife suspected of unfaithfulness.

chunks.title: Law of the Nazirite Vow
chunks.reason: A complete legal section detailing the vows, restrictions, and offerings for a Nazirite.

chunks.title: The Priestly Blessing
chunks.reason: A distinct speech where God instructs Moses to give a specific blessing formula to Aaron and his sons.

chunks.title: Instructions for the Lampstand
chunks.reason: Contains Yahweh's command to Aaron regarding the lampstand and the narrative of its execution.

chunks.title: Consecration of the Levites
chunks.reason: Details the ritual cleansing, offerings, and dedication of the Levites to serve in the tent of meeting.

chunks.title: Levite Service Terms
chunks.reason: Establishes the age limits for Levite service and their duties after retirement.

chunks.title: First Passover in Sinai
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the initial celebration of Passover.

chunks.title: Passover for the Unclean and Sojourners
chunks.reason: Dialogue leading to a legal statute regarding delayed Passover observance.

chunks.title: Guidance by the Cloud
chunks.reason: Description of the cloud's movement directing the camp's travel.

chunks.title: Instructions for Silver Trumpets
chunks.reason: A complete legal instruction regarding the making and usage of trumpets for assembly, travel, war, and feasts.

chunks.title: Order of March from Sinai
chunks.reason: A detailed catalog describing the sequence in which the tribes and Levitical families set out from the wilderness.

chunks.title: Moses Invites Hobab
chunks.reason: A dialogue exchange between Moses and his father-in-law Hobab regarding their journey to the Promised Land.

chunks.title: The Ark Leads the Way
chunks.reason: A narrative account of the journey beginning with the ark leading and Moses reciting two short prayers.

chunks.title: Fire at Taberah
chunks.reason: Narrative of the people complaining and God's fire consuming the outskirts.

chunks.title: The Greedy Rabble
chunks.reason: Description of the craving for meat and the nature of the manna.

chunks.title: Moses' Complaint
chunks.reason: Moses' prayer to God expressing the burden of leadership.

chunks.title: God's Response and Promise
chunks.reason: God instructs Moses to gather elders and promises meat for a month.

chunks.title: The Seventy Elders
chunks.reason: Narrative of the Spirit resting on elders and the incident with Eldad and Medad.

chunks.title: Quail and Kibroth-hattaavah
chunks.reason: The arrival of quail, the people's gluttony, and the subsequent plague.

chunks.title: Miriam and Aaron challenge Moses
chunks.reason: Describes the complaint against Moses regarding his Cushite wife and introduces Moses' humility.

chunks.title: Yahweh's judgment on Miriam and Aaron
chunks.reason: Records the divine summons, Yahweh's speech distinguishing Moses from other prophets, and the resulting anger.

chunks.title: Miriam struck with leprosy and Aaron pleads
chunks.reason: Narrates the physical manifestation of Miriam's disease and Aaron's intercession to Moses.

chunks.title: Moses' prayer and resolution
chunks.reason: Contains Moses' prayer for healing, Yahweh's decree of seven days isolation, and the conclusion of the event.

chunks.title: The Command to Spy
chunks.reason: Yahweh instructs Moses to send spies and Moses executes the command.

chunks.title: Names of the Spies
chunks.reason: A complete catalog listing the twelve leaders sent from each tribe.

chunks.title: Moses' Instructions to Spies
chunks.reason: Moses gives specific directives on what to observe and collect.

chunks.title: The Journey of the Spies
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the spies traveling the land and gathering fruit.

chunks.title: The Spies' Report
chunks.reason: The group returns to report on the land, including Caleb's encouragement and the majority's fearful assessment.

chunks.title: The People's Rebellion and Moses' Intercession
chunks.reason: Describes the congregation's grumbling, the spies' reaction, and the initial divine appearance.

chunks.title: Yahweh's Judgment and Moses' Plea
chunks.reason: Contains the direct exchange between Yahweh and Moses regarding the fate of the people.

chunks.title: The Decree of Forty Years
chunks.reason: A formal declaration by Yahweh detailing the specific punishment for the generation.

chunks.title: Fate of the Spies
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the spies' deaths and the survival of Joshua and Caleb.

chunks.title: The Hasty Ascent to the Hill Country
chunks.reason: Describes the people's attempt to enter the land after the decree and their subsequent defeat.

chunks.title: Offerings for the Land
chunks.reason: A continuous speech detailing grain and drink offerings for various sacrifices.

chunks.title: Dough Contribution
chunks.reason: Instructions on raising a contribution offering from the first of the dough.

chunks.title: Unintentional and High-Handed Sins
chunks.reason: Distinctions between unintentional errors requiring sacrifice and high-handed sins requiring cutting off.

chunks.title: The Wood Gatherer
chunks.reason: A narrative account of a man gathering wood on the Sabbath and his execution.

chunks.title: Tassels as Reminders
chunks.reason: Final instructions to wear tassels to remember the commandments and be holy.

chunks.title: Korah's Rebellion Begins
chunks.reason: Describes the gathering of rebels and their initial challenge to Moses and Aaron.

chunks.title: Moses's Response to Korah
chunks.reason: Moses falls in prayer and delivers a speech challenging the rebels to a test of holiness.

chunks.title: Dathan and Abiram Refuse
chunks.reason: Moses summons the Reubenites who reply with a sharp refusal and complaint.

chunks.title: The Test of Censers
chunks.reason: Moses sets the terms for the test and Yahweh appears to confirm the confrontation.

chunks.title: Judgment on the Rebels
chunks.reason: Moses predicts the judgment and the earth swallows the rebels while fire consumes the 250 men.

chunks.title: Instructions for the Censers
chunks.reason: Yahweh gives specific instructions to Eleazar regarding the bronze censers as a memorial.

chunks.title: The Plague and Aaron's Atonement
chunks.reason: The congregation grumbles again, a plague starts, and Aaron stops it with incense.

chunks.title: Instruction for Rods
chunks.reason: Yahweh commands Moses to gather rods and deposit them in the tent.

chunks.title: Aaron's Rod Sprouts
chunks.reason: The narrative describes the miracle of Aaron's rod blooming and Yahweh's command to preserve it.

chunks.title: Israel's Fear
chunks.reason: Moses obeys the command and the people respond with fear and questioning.

chunks.title: Instructions to Aaron and the Levites
chunks.reason: Yahweh defines the distinct roles of the priests and Levites regarding the sanctuary service.

chunks.title: Priestly Portions and Offerings
chunks.reason: Detailed regulations concerning the holy gifts, tithes, and firstborn animals given to Aaron's household.

chunks.title: Levites as Inheritance
chunks.reason: Yahweh explains that the Levites receive the tithe as their inheritance instead of land.

chunks.title: Tithe of the Tithe
chunks.reason: Instructions for the Levites to offer a portion of their tithe to Aaron as a contribution.

chunks.title: The Red Heifer Ritual
chunks.reason: Establishes the statute for preparing the red heifer and its ashes.

chunks.title: Purification from Corpse Uncleanliness
chunks.reason: Details the seven-day purification process and consequences for neglect.

chunks.title: Water from the Rock at Meribah
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit covering the arrival at Kadesh, the water crisis, Moses striking the rock, and the resulting judgment.

chunks.title: Moses Requests Passage Through Edom
chunks.reason: A distinct narrative block focused on the diplomatic exchange and refusal by Edom.

chunks.title: Death of Aaron at Mount Hor
chunks.reason: A complete account of the transition to Mount Hor, the transfer of priesthood, and Aaron's death.

chunks.title: Conquest of Arad and Hormah
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit describing the conflict with the Canaanite king and the resulting vow.

chunks.title: The Fiery Serpents and Bronze Serpent
chunks.reason: A coherent narrative block covering the people's complaint, the plague of serpents, and the remedy.

chunks.title: Journey to Moab and Song of the Well
chunks.reason: A travel itinerary interspersed with a specific song celebrating the well.

chunks.title: Defeat of Sihon King of the Amorites
chunks.reason: A complete narrative arc involving diplomatic negotiation, battle, and the poetic record of victory.

chunks.title: Conquest of Jazer and Bashan
chunks.reason: A concluding narrative segment detailing the capture of Jazer and the defeat of Og.

chunks.title: Balak Sends for Balaam
chunks.reason: Describes the political situation and Balak's initial mission to hire Balaam.

chunks.title: First Divine Refusal
chunks.reason: Contains the first interaction where God forbids Balaam from going.

chunks.title: Second Invitation and Permission
chunks.reason: Balak sends better messengers and God grants conditional permission.

chunks.title: The Donkey and the Angel
chunks.reason: Narrates the journey where the donkey sees the angel and speaks.

chunks.title: Arrival at Moab
chunks.reason: Describes Balaam's arrival, meeting with Balak, and the feast preparation.

chunks.title: First Oracle and Blessing
chunks.reason: Contains the preparation of altars, Balaam's first oracle delivered to Balak, and Balak's frustrated response.

chunks.title: Second Oracle and Blessing
chunks.reason: Describes the move to Pisgah, Balaam's second oracle emphasizing God's faithfulness, and Balak's complaint.

chunks.title: Third Oracle Setup
chunks.reason: Narrates the move to Peor and the final preparation of altars for a third attempt.

chunks.title: Balaam's Third Oracle on Israel
chunks.reason: A complete prophetic blessing describing Israel's prosperity and victory.

chunks.title: Balak's Anger and Balaam's Response
chunks.reason: A narrative exchange where Balak complains and Balaam explains his constraint.

chunks.title: Balaam's Fourth Oracle on the Messiah
chunks.reason: A distinct prophetic vision concerning a future ruler from Jacob.

chunks.title: Oracles Against Amalek, Kenite, and Kittim
chunks.reason: A series of short prophetic judgments against specific nations.

chunks.title: Departure of Balaam and Balak
chunks.reason: The concluding narrative action ending the chapter.

chunks.title: Idolatry at Shittim and the Plague
chunks.reason: Describes the Israelites' idolatry with Moabite women, the resulting plague, and Phinehas's intervention to stop it.

chunks.title: Covenant of Peace for Phinehas
chunks.reason: Combines Yahweh's speech granting a perpetual priesthood to Phinehas with specific identification of the slain couple.

chunks.title: Command Against Midian
chunks.reason: Yahweh commands Moses to be hostile toward the Midianites due to their role in the Peor incident.

chunks.title: Command to Take Census
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of the census command and location.

chunks.title: Census of Reuben, Simeon, and Gad
chunks.reason: Genealogical listing and population counts for the first three tribes.

chunks.title: Census of Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun
chunks.reason: Genealogical listing and population counts for the next three tribes.

chunks.title: Census of Joseph (Manasseh and Ephraim)
chunks.reason: Genealogical listing and population counts for the two tribes of Joseph.

chunks.title: Census of Benjamin, Dan, Asher, and Naphtali
chunks.reason: Genealogical listing and population counts for the remaining tribes.

chunks.title: Instructions for Land Division
chunks.reason: Divine instruction on how to distribute the land based on census numbers.

chunks.title: Census of the Levites
chunks.reason: Genealogical listing and population count for the tribe of Levi.

chunks.title: Summary of Census and Generational Shift
chunks.reason: Concluding narrative noting the new census location and the passing of the previous generation.

chunks.title: Daughters of Zelophehad Present Case
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of the daughters and their petition to Moses.

chunks.title: Inheritance Law for Daughters
chunks.reason: Divine ruling establishing inheritance succession when a man dies without sons.

chunks.title: Moses to See the Land
chunks.reason: God commands Moses to view the Promised Land before his death.

chunks.title: Moses Prays for a Successor
chunks.reason: Moses petitions God to appoint a leader for the congregation.

chunks.title: Appointment of Joshua
chunks.reason: God instructs Moses on how to commission Joshua as leader.

chunks.title: Joshua Commissioned
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion of Moses fulfilling God's command to install Joshua.

chunks.title: Daily Burnt Offering
chunks.reason: Establishes the routine morning and evening sacrifice instructions.

chunks.title: Sabbath Offerings
chunks.reason: Specifies the additional sacrifices required for the weekly Sabbath.

chunks.title: Monthly Offerings
chunks.reason: Details the burnt and sin offerings for the beginning of each month.

chunks.title: Feast of Unleavened Bread
chunks.reason: Outlines the sacrifices for Passover and the seven-day feast.

chunks.title: Feast of Weeks
chunks.reason: Describes the offerings for the Feast of Weeks and its holy convocation.

chunks.title: Feast of Trumpets Offerings
chunks.reason: Describes the specific sacrifices for the first day of the seventh month.

chunks.title: Day of Atonement Offerings
chunks.reason: Details the sacrifices for the tenth day of the seventh month.

chunks.title: Feast of Tabernacles Offerings
chunks.reason: Catalogs the daily sacrifices for the seven days of the feast and the eighth day.

chunks.title: Summary and Conclusion
chunks.reason: Summarizes the offerings and records Moses speaking to Israel.

chunks.title: Moses Introduces the Law on Vows
chunks.reason: Moses addresses the tribal heads to introduce Yahweh's command regarding vows.

chunks.title: Vows of a Young Woman in Her Father's House
chunks.reason: Details the conditions under which a father can confirm or annul his daughter's vows.

chunks.title: Vows of a Married Woman and Widow
chunks.reason: Describes how a husband or widow's status affects the validity of her vows.

chunks.title: Vows Made in a Husband's House
chunks.reason: Explains the husband's authority to annul vows made by his wife and the consequences of silence.

chunks.title: Conclusion of the Statutes
chunks.reason: Summarizes the chapter as statutes commanded by Yahweh to Moses regarding family relationships.

chunks.title: War Against Midian and Return
chunks.reason: Narrative of the battle command, execution, and return of the army with spoils.

chunks.title: Moses' Rebuke and Purification Laws
chunks.reason: Dialogue between Moses and the officers regarding the women, followed by instructions on ritual purification.

chunks.title: Distribution of Spoils
chunks.reason: Divine command and execution regarding the division of loot between warriors and the congregation.

chunks.title: Inventory of Spoils and Levies
chunks.reason: Detailed numerical breakdown of the livestock and people captured and the specific levies given to the priest and Levites.

chunks.title: Officers' Gold Offering
chunks.reason: Narrative of the army officers presenting gold to Moses as an offering for atonement.

chunks.title: Request for Land East of Jordan
chunks.reason: The sons of Reuben and Gad propose receiving land east of the Jordan as their inheritance.

chunks.title: Moses' Rebuke and Warning
chunks.reason: Moses confronts the tribes with a historical parallel to their fathers' failure at Kadesh-barnea.

chunks.title: Proposed Compromise
chunks.reason: The tribes offer to fight alongside Israel while keeping their families and livestock in the east.

chunks.title: Moses' Conditions
chunks.reason: Moses sets specific conditions for their request and warns of the consequences if they fail.

chunks.title: Agreement and Final Terms
chunks.reason: The tribes accept Moses' terms, and he instructs the leaders to formalize the agreement.

chunks.title: Final Oath of Loyalty
chunks.reason: The tribes reaffirm their commitment to cross the Jordan armed before Yahweh.

chunks.title: Granting of Land and Settlement
chunks.reason: Moses grants the land to the tribes, followed by a list of cities built by Gad, Reuben, Manasseh, Jair, and Nobah.

chunks.title: Introduction to the Journeys
chunks.reason: Introductory statement establishing Moses' record of Israel's journey from Egypt.

chunks.title: The Journey Log and Final Instructions
chunks.reason: A continuous list of camp locations from Rameses to the plains of Moab, concluding with Yahweh's speech to Moses regarding the conquest of Canaan.

chunks.title: Borders of the Land
chunks.reason: Yahweh's speech to Moses defining the four borders of Canaan.

chunks.title: Inheritance Distribution
chunks.reason: Moses explaining that the land is for the nine and a half tribes.

chunks.title: Leaders for Apportioning Land
chunks.reason: Yahweh's speech naming the leaders and priests responsible for dividing the land.

chunks.title: Cities for the Levites
chunks.reason: Divine instruction regarding the allocation of cities and pasture lands to the Levites.

chunks.title: Cities of Refuge and Legal Judgments
chunks.reason: Detailed statutes concerning the selection of cities of refuge, distinctions between accidental and intentional killing, and judicial procedures.

chunks.title: Petition of Gilead's Families
chunks.reason: The heads of the families present their concern regarding inheritance loss if Zelophehad's daughters marry outside their tribe.

chunks.title: Moses' Decree on Inheritance
chunks.reason: Moses delivers Yahweh's command establishing the rule that daughters inheriting land must marry within their father's tribe.

chunks.title: Fulfillment of the Decree
chunks.reason: The narrative describes how the daughters of Zelophehad obeyed the command by marrying their cousins.

chunks.title: Chapter Summary
chunks.reason: A concluding statement summarizing the commandments given by Yahweh to Israel through Moses in Moab.
